human_verification.notes: Validated financial-crimes-operations boundaries, FinCEN/FFIEC/CFPB/OFAC/Nacha/FDIC source grounding, suspicious-activity and consumer-impact escalation, unsupported override refusal, functionality-map completeness, and validated deployment and commercialization completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to financial-crimes operational handling of suspicious payment, sanctions, KYC/CIP, customer-impact, posting, and reconciliation cases rather than generic fraud adjudication or legal authority.

Authority sources anchor the pack in FinCEN rules and guidance, FFIEC examination procedures, CFPB consumer-protection guidance, OFAC sanctions controls, Nacha ACH workflow rules, and FDIC operational-control standards.

Scenarios validate unsupported financial changes, missing documentation, reconciliation mismatches, suspicious-activity escalation, boundary-safe refusals, conflicting-source handling, and tenant-specific adaptation.
The package requires entity type, product line, jurisdiction, systems of record, approval thresholds, and authoritative transaction or case records before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of transaction, screening, case, communication, and reconciliation records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
